title: Amazon Dethrones Walmart as America's Largest Company
content:
In the modern corporate landscape, true success at scale requires persuading a massive consumer base that a company consistently delivers the goods. Amazon perfectly understands this critical lesson, utilizing its massive reach to become the newly crowned leader of the Fortune 500. The e-commerce giant's ability to attract and effectively monetize the masses has become a virtually unstoppable economic force. Consequently, Amazon has finally toppled Walmart, ending the retail competitor's impressive thirteen-year streak atop the ranking of largest U.S. companies by revenue. Furthermore, Amazon delivered record-breaking profits this year, largely driven by the explosive growth of its highly popular AWS cloud computing business.

The broader business world remains engaged in an eternal battle to keep demanding customers fully satisfied and loyal. For example, tech behemoth Microsoft is currently scrambling to secure the fierce loyalty of new artificial intelligence users. Similarly, financial institutions like American Express had to completely reimagine their traditional credit card perks to guide customers through the global pandemic. However, even the most customer-obsessed companies inevitably stumble and face significant public relations challenges from time to time. Ultimately, the true test of corporate resilience is how fallen industry giants manage to regain consumer trust after making highly publicized mistakes.
